status: first Telos repo mixed-surface consolidation pass
purpose: Classify the Telos repository's front-door, control-plane, research, proof-demo, outreach, receipt, integration, and quarantine surfaces without treating working-tree artifacts as shipped release state.
claim_boundary: This registry is based on current local reads plus live flagship receipts. It is not a release certification, site-publication proof, scientific result, market claim, or proof that all local changes are committed or pushed.

evidence.summary: Live flagship status tools returned MATCH before this subregistry was created.
Manifest records source-checkout profiles, package profiles, host targets, expected tool surfaces, and freshness probes for Gather, Index, Forum, Crucible, and Telos.
Gather sealed a docs digest and returned a bounded catalog, with large payloads dropped from model context.

untracked_families: QEC proof packet demo and source receipts

rows.role: public repository front door and release ledger
rows.current_action: Keep concise and link to subregistries; do not move detailed strategy or raw research payloads into the README.
rows.publication_boundary: Front-door docs should only claim stable public demos and current registry links.

rows.role: current-state, architecture, roadmap, posture, connection, and inventory docs
rows.current_action: Use as human-readable control plane; refresh after Index, Forum, Gather, and Crucible passes.
rows.publication_boundary: These docs can state strategy and scope, but must distinguish committed, working-tree, planned, inferred, and unverifiable claims.

rows.role: machine and human registries for cross-repo and subregistry classification
rows.current_action: Treat as the source of truth for documentation routing until superseded by newer dated registries.
rows.publication_boundary: Registries classify and route; they do not prove content correctness, publication readiness, or release status.

rows.role: public-safe official packets and longer paper drafts for frontier research lanes
rows.current_action: Route through the publication queue and require source, scope, falsification, reproduction, risk, and freshness gates.
rows.publication_boundary: Research docs are not field-result proof; each must retain non-claims for biology, QEC, robotics, PDE, TI Morse field intake, and BuildLang-native execution.

rows.role: runnable local fixtures, tests, and source receipts for bounded proof packets
rows.current_action: Keep fixtures paired with targeted tests and explicit non-claims; mark QEC as working-tree evidence until committed.
rows.publication_boundary: Fixture MATCH does not imply real-world QEC, robotics, medical, industrial, causal-discovery, or BuildLang-native claims.

rows.role: social, comment, and wave outreach drafts
rows.current_action: Keep separated from official claims; only cite public artifacts that are verified and live.
rows.publication_boundary: Outreach copy is not evidence. It can summarize a claim only after the official packet, proof demo, and receipt are ready.

rows.role: run receipts, measurements, theses, reports, Learn packets, and Crucible registry objects
rows.current_action: Use hashes, reports, and typed receipt refs in public docs; keep raw object stores and run internals out of front-door copy.
rows.publication_boundary: Receipts support bounded claims only when the referenced packet and verifier are in scope.

rows.role: CLI demos, MCP tool catalog, host manifests, research seed adapters, and capability tests
rows.current_action: Treat tested demos as implementation evidence only for their bounded contracts; keep catalog and manifest parity validated.
rows.publication_boundary: A demo command proving a contract does not prove external service integration, production readiness, or scientific field results.

rows.role: frontier R&D scope and high-risk publication boundary
rows.current_action: Use to permit broad frontier R&D scope while requiring public-safe shapes for cybernetics, biochem, nuclear, AI/ML, quant, defense-adjacent, medical, and robotics material.
rows.publication_boundary: Public docs should emphasize research, measurement, safety, simulation, assurance, education, and accountable tooling rather than operational instructions.

rows.role: implementation plans, specs, and verification reports
rows.current_action: Reference as implementation lineage and verification evidence only when the matching demo or report is current.
rows.publication_boundary: Specs and historical verification reports can drift from current behavior; rerun relevant commands before making capability claims.

rows.current_action: Keep budget and presentation docs factual and separate from scientific proof or market uniqueness claims.
rows.publication_boundary: Budget and brand docs are operational context, not proof of traction, funding, or market validation.

rows.role: caches, local Gather payloads, temporary data, and ignored source-like material
rows.current_action: Do not copy raw payloads, caches, private paths, or local run state into public docs.
rows.publication_boundary: Public artifacts may refer to digest seals, redacted refs, and claim boundaries only.

publication_controls: Working-tree artifacts must be labeled working-tree-only until committed or explicitly published.
Outreach copy must not become evidence; official packets and proof demos come first.
Receipt object stores should be cited by digest, report, and typed packet reference rather than copied into front-door docs.
Raw transcripts, caches, and private/local payloads remain internal-source or quarantine-and-adapt.
Every research packet needs source, scope, falsification, reproduction, risk, and freshness gates before public promotion.
Every demo claim is bounded to the command, fixture, and negative controls that were actually run.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Commit-boundary publication gate
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Add a check that blocks README/site claims when the referenced demo or doc is still working-tree-only.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Receipt-index bridge
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Feed docs/outreach/receipts and demo/research receipts into Repo Proof Index or a Telos proof index row.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Outreach-to-official claim gate
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Require each outreach wave to cite an official packet, proof-demo command, and receipt id.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Cache and raw payload quarantine
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Add a stale/raw payload scan that flags docs/research/dogfood caches and local Gather payload references.

next_actions: Keep this Telos repo subregistry synchronized with each documentation consolidation pass.
Create one row per public site page once the portfolio site QEC and proof-demo pages are committed and live.
Build a receipt-index bridge over docs/outreach/receipts and demo/research receipts.
Add a commit-boundary check before README, site, or outreach copy says a working-tree artifact is shipped.
Review docs/research/dogfood cache files and ignore policy so cache artifacts cannot pollute public research evidence.
